LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT 21ST CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: OVERRULED. DEFENDANT MAY BE LIABLE FOR UNREASONABLE DELAY IN PROCESSING A CLAIM. HAILEY, 158 CAL. APP. 4TH 452. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES AN UNJUSTIFIED DELAY OF OVER TWO YEARS. ACCORDINGLY IT STATES A CLAIM TO THAT EXTENT. JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT 21ST CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. GRANTED AS UNDISPUTED REGARDING PRAYER FOR COSTS, INTEREST, AND ALLEGATIONS REGARDING DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED REFUSAL TO PAY THE AWARD. DENIED AS TO THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND PRAYER FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES: THE ASSERTED BASIS OF INVASION OF PRIVACY IN DEMANDING PRIVATE MEDICAL RECORDS WITHOUT CAUSE MAY SUPPORT THE CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
